UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, linked to their authentication identity. This entity stores details about the farmer or administrator using the TUAI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. (Primary Key)

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication (e.g., OTP login) and communication.

displayName.description: The user's preferred name to be displayed in the application.

phone.description: The user's phone number, primarily for notification purposes. Optional.

countryCode.description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code associated with the user, e.g., 'MY' for Malaysia. Used for regional data tailoring.

language.description: The preferred language of the user, e.g., 'en-US', 'ms-MY'. Used for localization.

farmIds.description: References to Farm entities that this user is associated with. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Farm)

Farm.title: Farm
Farm.description: Represents a specific farm owned or managed by a user. It includes geographical and descriptive details about the agricultural land.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Farm entity. (Primary Key)

description.description: A brief description of the farm.

addressText.description: The human-readable address of the farm.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the farm's location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the farm's location.

farmSizeValue.description: The numerical value of the farm's size.

farmSizeUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the farm's size (e.g., 'hectares', 'acres').

userIds.description: References to UserProfile entities that are associated with managing this farm. (Relationship: Farm N:N UserProfile)

CropRecord.title: CropRecord
CropRecord.description: Stores details about a specific crop planting or batch on a farm, including its type, planting date, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CropRecord entity. (Primary Key)

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this crop is planted. (Relationship: Farm 1:N CropRecord)

cropType.description: The type of crop being cultivated (e.g., 'Padi', 'Sawit', 'Sayur').

variety.description: The specific variety of the crop (e.g., 'MR219'). Optional.

plantingDate.description: The date and time when the crop was planted.

harvestDate.description: The planned or actual date and time of the crop harvest. Optional.

status.description: The current status of the crop (e.g., 'Planned', 'Planted', 'Growing', 'Harvested', 'Completed').

notes.description: Additional notes or observations about this crop record. Optional.

FarmActivity.title: FarmActivity
FarmActivity.description: Logs various activities performed on a farm or for a specific crop record, such as fertilization, pest control, or harvest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FarmActivity entity. (Primary Key)

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm where this activity took place. (Relationship: Farm 1:N FarmActivity)

cropRecordId.description: Reference to the CropRecord related to this activity. Optional, if activity is farm-general. (Relationship: CropRecord 1:N FarmActivity)

activityType.description: The type of activity performed (e.g., 'Planting', 'Fertilization', 'Pest Control', 'Irrigation', 'Harvest', 'Observation').

activityDate.description: The date and time when the activity was performed.

description.description: A detailed description of the activity.

materialsUsed.description: Description of materials used during the activity (e.g., 'Urea fertilizer 50kg'). Optional.

cost.description: The cost associated with this activity. Optional.

notes.description: Additional notes about the activity. Optional.

CropScanResult.title: CropScanResult
CropScanResult.description: Stores the results of AI-powered crop image analysis for disease identification and recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CropScanResult entity. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the scan. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CropScanResult)

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm associated with the scanned crop. Optional. (Relationship: Farm 1:N CropScanResult)

cropRecordId.description: Reference to the CropRecord if the scan is specific to a planted crop batch. Optional. (Relationship: CropRecord 1:N CropScanResult)

imageStoragePath.description: The path or URL to the uploaded crop image in Firebase Storage.

scanDate.description: The date and time when the crop image was scanned.

diseaseIdentified.description: The name of the disease identified by the AI, or a summary if multiple are found.

confidenceScore.description: A numerical score indicating the AI's confidence in the disease identification (0.0 to 1.0). Optional.

recommendations.description: AI-generated recommendations for treating the identified disease.

status.description: The processing status of the scan (e.g., 'Processed', 'Pending Review').

Alert.title: Alert
Alert.description: Represents an alert or notification generated by the AI system, potentially targeted to users or farms, providing timely information or actions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alert entity. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile targeted by this alert. Optional, if the alert is broadcast. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Alert)

farmId.description: Reference to the Farm targeted by this alert. Optional, if the alert is user-level or broadcast. (Relationship: Farm 1:N Alert)

title.description: A concise title for the alert.

message.description: The main content or body of the alert message.

severity.description: The severity level of the alert (e.g., 'Info', 'Warning', 'Critical').

issuedDate.description: The date and time when the alert was issued.

validUntilDate.description: The date and time until which the alert is considered valid. Optional.

region.description: The geographical region relevant to this alert (e.g., 'Malaysia-Kedah', 'ASEAN').

cropAffected.description: The specific crop type affected by this alert (e.g., 'Padi', 'All'). Optional.

recommendedActions.description: A list of actionable steps recommended by the AI in response to the alert.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the user has read this alert.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Contains information about agricultural suppliers, including their contact details, location, and products offered, used for the 'Nearby Supplier Finder' feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity. (Primary Key)

name.description: The name of the supplier or business.

contactPerson.description: The name of a primary contact person at the supplier. Optional.

email.description: The email address of the supplier. Optional.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the supplier. Optional.

website.description: The official website URL of the supplier. Optional.

addressText.description: The human-readable physical address of the supplier.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the supplier's location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the supplier's location.

productsServices.description: A list of products or services offered by the supplier (e.g., 'Fertilizer', 'Seeds', 'Pesticides', 'Farm Equipment').

notes.description: Any additional notes or internal information about the supplier. Optional.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Stores the history of a conversation session with the Farmer Copilot Assistant, including messages exchanged between the user and the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who participated in this chat session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatSession)

startTime.description: The date and time when the chat session started.

endTime.description: The date and time when the chat session ended. Optional.

topic.description: A brief summary or primary topic of the chat session (e.g., 'fertilizer advice', 'crop disease query').

messages.description: An ordered list of messages exchanged within this chat session.

definition.description: Stores user profile data, private to the user. The document ID `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`. Each document in this collection represents a UserProfile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores collaborative farm details. Includes a 'members' map (e.g., {uid1: 'owner', uid2: 'viewer'}) for explicit role-based authorization. Access is restricted to users listed in this map, enabling multiple users to manage a single farm.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique ID of the farm.

definition.description: Stores records of crop plantings specific to a farm. Includes denormalized 'farmMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Farm document's 'members' map.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent farm.

params.name: cropRecordId
params.description: The unique ID of the crop record.

definition.description: Logs activities performed on a farm. Includes denormalized 'farmMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Farm document's 'members' map.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent farm.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique ID of the farm activity.

definition.description: Stores results of AI crop image analysis, private to the user who initiated the scan. The document ID `{cropScanResultId}` corresponds to the `id` field within the entity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who performed the scan, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: cropScanResultId
params.description: The unique ID of the crop scan result.

definition.description: Stores alerts specifically targeted to a user. Access is private to the user, where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user targeted by the alert, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique ID of the alert.

definition.description: Stores alerts specifically targeted to a farm. Includes denormalized 'farmMembers' map for authorization independence, copied from its parent Farm document's 'members' map.

params.name: farmId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent farm.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique ID of the alert.

definition.description: Stores public or region-wide alerts, readable by any authenticated user. Writing would typically be restricted to administrators. Client-side filtering by region is expected for display.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique ID of the alert.

definition.description: Stores information about agricultural suppliers. Publicly readable by authenticated users; write access typically restricted to administrators. Client-side filtering for 'Nearby Supplier Finder' is expected.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique ID of the supplier.

definition.description: Stores chat sessions with the AI copilot, private to the user. The document ID `{chatSessionId}` corresponds to the `id` field within the entity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who initiated the chat session, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique ID of the chat session.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. This structure leverages path-based authorization for user-centric data, and denormalized membership maps for collaborative data, while segregating data with different security postures into distinct collections.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Collaborative Farm Data:** For entities like `Farm`, which are collaborative (managed by multiple users), a top-level collection `/farms/{farmId}` is used. Each `Farm` document will include a `members` map (e.g., `{'uid1': 'owner', 'uid2': 'viewer'}`) to explicitly define user roles and access. Crucially, for subcollections of `Farm` (e.g., `/farms/{farmId}/cropRecords`, `/farms/{farmId}/farmActivities`, `/farms/{farmId}/alerts`), the `members` map from the parent `Farm` document is **denormalized** and copied into `resource.data.farmMembers` within each child document. This eliminates the need for security rules to perform `get()` operations on the parent `Farm` document, making rules atomic, simpler (`request.auth.uid in resource.data.farmMembers`), and highly debuggable. This approach ensures that child documents can be created or updated independently without needing to read the parent, enabling robust transactions and batch writes.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **Private User Data:** For collections organized under `/users/{userId}/...` (e.g., `UserProfile`, `ChatSession`, `CropScanResult`, user-specific `Alerts`), security rules enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This inherently supports `list` operations (QAPs) because any query for these subcollections is already scoped to the authenticated user's private data, and the rules guarantee only that user can access it.
2.  **Collaborative Farm Data:** Users can query for farms they are members of by using a `where` clause on the `members` map (e.g., `db.collection('farms').where('members.' + auth.uid, '==', 'owner')`). Once a `farmId` is obtained, `list` operations on subcollections like `cropRecords` or `farmActivities` are secure because each document in these subcollections contains the denormalized `farmMembers` map. The rules then verify `request.auth.uid in resource.data.farmMembers`, ensuring that only authorized users can list or access documents within that farm's subcollections. This allows for efficient client-side filtering combined with robust server-side authorization.
3.  **Global/Public Data:** Collections like `/suppliers` and `/publicAlerts` are designed for broad readability by authenticated users (`allow read: if request.auth != null;`). This allows users to perform `list` operations on the entire collection. Further filtering (e.g., by location for suppliers, by region for public alerts) is expected to occur client-side. The security rules here are not designed to filter content but to define the baseline access permissions for the collection, supporting QAPs by allowing full listing to authorized clients.
